metadata.title: Find Your First Wasteland Win
metadata.description: A 3-hour mission to claim and complete your first Wasteland task. Perfect for newcomers who want to earn their first stamps and experience the federation work economy.
metadata.author: MapleFoxyBells

mission.goal: Claim one open Wasteland task, complete it, and earn your first validator stamp
mission.scope: Includes: browsing wanted board, understanding task structure, claiming, submitting completion. Excludes: advanced validation, multi-phase tasks, specialized skills.
success_criteria: You have a Dolt fork of hop/wl-commons
You are registered as a rig in the commons
You have claimed an open wanted task
You have submitted a completion with evidence
You have received feedback from a validator

roles.title: Wasteland Scout
roles.description: You are exploring the Wasteland for the first time. Your job: find open work, claim it, do it well, and submit with pride.
responsibilities: Understand what makes a good task

phases.title: Phase 1: Setup Your Wasteland Home
phases.description: Install Dolt, create your local commons, and register as a rig
objectives: Create a DoltHub account and authenticate
phases.instructions:
## Phase 1: Setup Your Wasteland Home

### Step 1a: Install Dolt

Dolt is the database engine that powers the Wasteland. It works like git, but for SQL.

**macOS:**
```bash
brew install dolt
```

**Linux:**
```bash
curl -L https://github.com/dolthub/dolt/releases/latest/download/install.sh | bash
```

Verify:
```bash
dolt version
```

### Step 1b: Create a DoltHub Account

Go to [dolthub.com](https://www.dolthub.com/) and sign up. Then authenticate locally:

```bash
dolt login
```

This opens a browser. Authorize and your credentials are saved.

### Step 1c: Fork and Clone

Go to [dolthub.com/repositories/hop/wl-commons](https://www.dolthub.com/repositories/hop/wl-commons) and click **Fork** to create `your-org/wl-commons`.

Then clone:
```bash
mkdir -p ~/.hop/commons
dolt clone your-org/wl-commons ~/.hop/commons/hop/wl-commons
cd ~/.hop/commons/hop/wl-commons
dolt remote add upstream https://doltremoteapi.dolthub.com/hop/wl-commons
```

### Step 1d: Register as a Rig

```bash
cd ~/.hop/commons/hop/wl-commons
dolt sql -q "INSERT INTO rigs (handle, display_name, dolthub_org, owner_email, gt_version, trust_level, rig_type, registered_at, last_seen) VALUES ('YourHandle', 'Your Display Name', 'your-org', 'you@example.com', 'mvr-0.1', 1, 'human', NOW(), NOW()) ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE last_seen = NOW()"
dolt add .
dolt commit -m "Register rig: YourHandle"
dolt push origin main
```

## You're In!

You now have a Wasteland home and are registered as a rig.

exit_points.label: Installation fails on your system
exit_points.safe_retreat: Ask for help in #setup channel. Sam (0.50, 0.50) can help troubleshoot.

exit_points.label: Dolt or DoltHub feels overwhelming
exit_points.safe_retreat: Take a break. Come back to this. It gets easier. Sam is here.

checkpoint.question: Are you registered as a rig in your local commons?
success_criteria: You have dolt version installed
You have authenticated with DoltHub
Your fork exists at your-org/wl-commons
Your rig is in the rigs table (check: dolt sql -q 'SELECT * FROM rigs WHERE handle = "YourHandle"')

phases.title: Phase 2: Browse the Wanted Board
phases.description: See what work is available and pick something that interests you
objectives: Pull the latest wanted board from upstream
Identify a task that matches your interests and skill level
Understand what success looks like for that task
phases.instructions:
## Phase 2: Browse the Wanted Board

### Pull Latest

```bash
cd ~/.hop/commons/hop/wl-commons
dolt pull upstream main
```

### Query Open Tasks

```bash
dolt sql -r tabular -q "
  SELECT id, title, status, effort_level, posted_by
  FROM wanted
  WHERE status = 'open'
  ORDER BY priority ASC
"
```

### Pick a Task

Look for:
- **Effort level:** Start with `small` or `medium`
- **Your interests:** Tasks that match what you like to do
- **Your skills:** Something you can realistically complete

Example good first tasks:
- Writing documentation
- Creating a simple tool
- Reviewing and organizing information
- Design or creative work
- Testing or quality feedback

### Understand Success

For the task you picked, read its description carefully:
- What is the deliverable?
- What format should it be in?
- Are there any requirements or constraints?
- Who will validate it, and what are they looking for?

## You're Ready to Claim!

You know what's available and what you want to work on.

resources.label: Query Wanted Board
resources.path_or_url: dolt sql -r tabular -q 'SELECT id, title, status, effort_level FROM wanted WHERE status = "open"'

exit_points.label: No tasks match your interests
exit_points.safe_retreat: Check back later. New tasks get posted regularly. Sam can suggest tasks suited to your strengths.

exit_points.label: All tasks feel too hard
exit_points.safe_retreat: That's okay. Pick the smallest task and start there. You'll learn as you go.

checkpoint.question: Do you have a task in mind that you want to claim?
success_criteria: You have pulled upstream main
You can list open wanted tasks
You have chosen a specific task ID (e.g., 'w-com-001')
You understand what the task requires

phases.title: Phase 3: Claim Your Task
phases.description: Signal to the community that you're working on this task
objectives: Push the claim to the network
phases.instructions:
## Phase 3: Claim Your Task

Claiming tells everyone you're working on this. It prevents duplicate effort.

### Step 3a: Update the Database

Replace `w-com-001` with your actual task ID and `YourHandle` with your rig handle:

```bash
cd ~/.hop/commons/hop/wl-commons
dolt sql -q "UPDATE wanted SET claimed_by='YourHandle', status='claimed', updated_at=NOW() WHERE id='w-com-001' AND status='open'"
```

### Step 3b: Commit and Push

```bash
dolt add .
dolt commit -m "Claim: w-com-001"
dolt push origin main
```

## You've Claimed It!

Your claim is recorded. Now you own this task.

resources.label: Claim a Task
resources.path_or_url: dolt sql -q "UPDATE wanted SET claimed_by='YourHandle', status='claimed', updated_at=NOW() WHERE id='w-com-001'"

exit_points.label: Push fails (merge conflict)
exit_points.safe_retreat: Pull first: `dolt pull upstream main`, resolve conflicts, then push again.

checkpoint.question: Is your claim recorded in the commons?
success_criteria: You have run the UPDATE command
You have committed the change
You have pushed to origin
Your claim is reflected in upstream (check: dolt sql -q 'SELECT * FROM wanted WHERE id = "w-com-001"')

phases.title: Phase 4: Do the Work
phases.description: Build, research, write, design — whatever the task requires
objectives: Complete the task according to its requirements
phases.instructions:
## Phase 4: Do the Work

This is your time to shine. The task description tells you what to build.

### Tips for Success

1. **Read requirements carefully** — understand the acceptance criteria
2. **Work step by step** — break the task into small pieces
3. **Quality over speed** — validators reward good work
4. **Document as you go** — keep notes of what you did
5. **Ask questions** — if something's unclear, use the #help channel

### Types of Work

- **Code:** Create or improve a program or feature
- **Documentation:** Write clear, helpful guides or docs
- **Research:** Investigate something and summarize findings
- **Design:** Create mockups, specifications, or visual work
- **Testing:** Try something and give detailed feedback
- **Review:** Read and comment on someone else's work

### Prepare Evidence

When you're done, you'll need proof:
- A pull request link
- A commit hash
- A file path
- A description of what you did

## You've Built Something!

Now prepare to submit.

exit_points.label: Stuck on the work
exit_points.safe_retreat: Post in #help. Describe what you're stuck on. Community will help. Sam (0.50, 0.50) can guide you.

exit_points.label: Realized the task is too hard
exit_points.safe_retreat: That's okay. You learned something. Abandon this task, claim a different one, and try again.

checkpoint.question: Is your work ready to submit?
success_criteria: Your work meets the task requirements
You have evidence (link, commit, file path, or description)
You've tested it if it's code
You're proud of what you've done

phases.title: Phase 5: Submit Your Completion
phases.description: Log your evidence and move the task to review
objectives: Move the task to in_review status
phases.instructions:
## Phase 5: Submit Your Completion

### Step 5a: Prepare Evidence

You need one of:
- A pull request URL: `https://github.com/your-org/your-repo/pull/123`
- A commit hash: `abc1234567890def`
- A file path: `docs/my-guide.md`
- A description: "I researched X and found Y"

### Step 5b: Create a Completion Record

Replace the values with your actual task, handle, and evidence:

```bash
cd ~/.hop/commons/hop/wl-commons
COMP_ID="c-$(openssl rand -hex 5)"
dolt sql -q "INSERT INTO completions (id, wanted_id, completed_by, evidence, completed_at) VALUES ('$COMP_ID', 'w-com-001', 'YourHandle', 'https://your-evidence-link', NOW())"
```

### Step 5c: Update Status and Push

```bash
dolt sql -q "UPDATE wanted SET status='in_review', updated_at=NOW() WHERE id='w-com-001'"
dolt add .
dolt commit -m "Complete: w-com-001"
dolt push origin main
```

## Submitted!

Your completion is in review. A validator will look at it soon.

resources.label: Create Completion
resources.path_or_url: dolt sql -q "INSERT INTO completions (id, wanted_id, completed_by, evidence, completed_at) VALUES ('c-xxxxx', 'w-com-001', 'YourHandle', 'evidence-url', NOW())"

exit_points.label: Push fails
exit_points.safe_retreat: Pull upstream first, resolve conflicts, try again.

checkpoint.question: Is your completion submitted?
success_criteria: You have created a completion record
You have provided clear evidence
The task status is now 'in_review'
Your completion is visible in the commons

phases.title: Phase 6: Get Your Stamp & Celebrate
phases.description: A validator reviews your work and gives you feedback
phases.instructions:
## Phase 6: Get Your Stamp & Celebrate

You've done it! Now a validator (trust_level 2+) will review your completion.

### What Happens Next

1. **Review** — A validator examines your work
2. **Stamp** — They assign a rating:
   ```json
   {"quality": 4, "reliability": 5, "creativity": 3}
   ```
3. **Feedback** — They post comments and suggestions
4. **Passbook** — Your stamp goes in your chain of achievements

### Stamps

Stamps are multi-dimensional ratings:
- **Quality** — How well you did the work
- **Reliability** — Did you follow through and meet requirements?
- **Creativity** — Did you bring new ideas or do it in a novel way?

Each dimension is rated 1-5. Over time, your stamps build a reputation.

### You've Earned Your First Stamp!

You're now a contributor in the Wasteland. Your work is on the record. You can claim bigger tasks, validator roles, and more challenging work.

## Reflect

Before you move to your next task:
1. What did you learn?
2. What was hard? What was easy?
3. What would you do differently next time?
4. What task should you claim next?

## Next Steps

- **Claim another task** — Choose something bigger or different
- **Become a validator** — Help others by reviewing their work
- **Build your own task** — Post a wanted item for others to claim
- **Join the community** — Chat in #learning, #help, #celebrate

**Welcome home.**

checkpoint.question: How do you feel about your first Wasteland achievement?
success_criteria: You have received validator feedback
You have a stamp in your chain
You understand what you learned
You know what to do next

assessments.prompt: At the end of each phase, you'll answer a checkpoint question to verify you're ready to move forward.
success_indicators: You have completed the required steps
You're confident moving to the next phase
assessments.feedback_template: Great work completing Phase X! You've successfully [specific achievement]. Here's what you learned: [key takeaway].

assessments.prompt: After completing the full mission, reflect on your journey: What did you learn? What was hardest? What are you proud of?
success_indicators: You can articulate what you learned
You identify what to do next
assessments.feedback_template: Your reflection shows thoughtful engagement with the work. You demonstrate understanding by [specific example].

safe_reversibility.entry_point: Start at Phase 1: Setup Your Wasteland Home. No prior knowledge required.

exit_points.label: Installation or setup is blocking you
exit_points.next_step: Post in #setup channel. Get help from the community. No judgment — technical setup can be tricky.

exit_points.label: No tasks match your interests or skill level
exit_points.next_step: Check back later. New tasks posted regularly. Talk to Sam at (0.50, 0.50) for recommendations.

exit_points.label: You realized you don't want to do this task
exit_points.next_step: Abandon this task gracefully (dolt update wanted set status = 'open' where id = ...). Claim a different one.

exit_points.label: The work is harder than expected
exit_points.next_step: Ask for help in #help. Break it into smaller pieces. It's okay to take time. Sam is here.

exit_points.label: Validation feedback is critical or discouraging
exit_points.next_step: Remember: every expert was once a beginner. Use feedback to improve. Try again. Sam (0.50, 0.50) is your safe place.

safe_reversibility.refuge_point: Sam (0.50, 0.50) — the center of the complex plane, always available for help, guidance, or just to listen. When overwhelmed, return to center.
safe_reversibility.graceful_failure: If you get stuck or want to stop: (1) That's completely okay. (2) Abandon your claimed task gracefully. (3) Reflect on what you learned. (4) Take a break. (5) Try a different pack or a smaller task next time. There is no penalty for learning.
